---
title: "Material overview"
---

## 📚 General references

The following textbooks offer a good general reference to the course
content, and I think its a good idea to read into these books in a 
general way. Moreover, I often point to chapters in the respective 
session pages.

- Wickham, H., Çetinkaya-Rundel, M., & Grolemund, G. (2023). *R for data science: Import, tidy, transform, visualize, and model data* (2nd edition). O'Reilly. [https://r4ds.hadley.nz/](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/)

- Ismay, C., & Kim, A. Y.-S. (2020). *Statistical inference via data science: A ModernDive, into R and the tidyverse*. CRC Press, Taylor and Francis Group. [https://moderndive.com/index.html](https://moderndive.com/index.html)

- Békés, G., & Kézdi, G. (2021). Data Analysis for Business, Economics, and Policy. Cambridge University Press. [https://gabors-data-analysis.com/](https://gabors-data-analysis.com/)

For more advanced details on the fundamentals of programming in R, I 
recommend the following:

- Wickham, H. (2019). *Advanced R* (Second edition). CRC Press/Taylor & Francis Group. [https://adv-r.hadley.nz/](https://adv-r.hadley.nz/)

For the model-related parts of the lecture I recommend the following book as a further reading reference:

- James, G., Witten, D., Hastie, T., & Tibshirani, R. (2021). An introduction to statistical learning: With applications in R (Second edition). Springer.  [https://www.statlearning.com/](https://www.statlearning.com/) 

## 🔖 Session-specific material

The implementation lab takes place on **Fridays** during the lecture period
**18 September – 11 December 2026**. The schedule below follows the official
seminar timetable; topics and dates are matched to the
[main lecture](/content/lecture/).

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## How to use the exercise codes?
For information on how to use the exercise code, read
[this tutorial](/content/tutorials/using-exercises/).
:::


|  Session  | Date        | Topic                                                              | Exercise code                            |
|:---------:|:-----------:|:-------------------------------------------------------------------|:----------------------------------------:|
|     1     | 18 Sep 2026 | [General introduction & installation](/content/material/s_01_Installation.qmd) |                                      |
|     2     | 25 Sep 2026 | [Basics of R and R Studio](/content/material/s_02_Basics.qmd)         | `Basics`, `Functions`                    |
|     3     | 02 Oct 2026 | [Project management & recap](/content/material/s_03_ProjectManagement.qmd)       | `ProjectOrga`                            |
|     4     | 09 Oct 2026 | [Object types](/content/material/s_04_ObjectTypes.qmd)                     | `ObjectTypes1`, `ObjectTypes2`           |
|     5     | 23 Oct 2026 | [Quarto and R Markdown](/content/material/s_05_Quarto.qmd)            | `Quarto`                                 |
|     6     | 30 Oct 2026 | [Importing data](/content/material/s_06_DataImport.qmd)                   | `Wrangling1`, `Wrangling2`               |
|     7     | 06 Nov 2026 | [Data preparation](/content/material/s_07_DataPreparation.qmd)                 | `Wrangling1`, `Wrangling2`               |
|     8     | 13 Nov 2026 | [Data visualization](/content/material/s_08_Visualization.qmd)              | `Visualization1`                         |
|     9     | 20 Nov 2026 | [Using AI for coding](/content/material/s_09_AICoding.qmd)              |                                          |
|    10     | 27 Nov 2026 | [Linear models in R](/content/material/s_10_LinearModels.qmd)              | `LinearRegression1`, `LinearRegression2` |
|    11     | 04 Dec 2026 | [Experimental data in R](/content/material/s_11_Experiments.qmd)           |                                          |
|    12     | 11 Dec 2026 | Recap session *(material TBA)*                                      |                                          |

: There is no class in the week of 12–16 October 2026. The three **take-home exams**
are handed out after sessions 5 (23 Oct), 8 (13 Nov) and 10 (27 Nov).

There are also complementary recap exercises on data import, preparation and
visualization in the [recap session](/content/material/s_08b_Recap.qmd).
